conjectures.name: C1: Cross-rotation kill (fixed-point-free transport at a resonance)
conjectures.statement: There exists a lineage chi-5 UDG (G,q) (e.g. S199 or P510, exact coords in Q(sqrt3,sqrt11)) and a rotation rho (algebraic center O, algebraic angle theta) with nonempty cross set R(rho) = {(u,v) in VxV : |q(u) - rho(q(v))| = 1}, such that EVERY proper 5-coloring c of G has some (u,v) in R(rho) with c(u) = c(v). Equivalently: the SAT instance [5-coloring constraints of G on ONE variable set x] + [x_u != x_v for all (u,v) in R(rho)] is UNSAT. This says the band-1 transport relation T on C_5(G) has no fixed point: no coloring survives being copied onto its own rotated image.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Compute the full resonance spectrum of (G, O): for each ordered pair (u,v), the angles theta with |q(u) - rho_theta(q(v))| = 1 (at most 2 per pair, by Euclidean two-circle intersection; solvable exactly by the e1e binding-rotation system already in the repo). Cluster angles that serve >= 2 pairs simultaneously. For each of the top ~100 resonant angles, run the diagonal SAT instance above (|V| color variables, sub-second). Prediction: at least one UNSAT. Refutation-for-lineage: all instances SAT across the exhaustive resonance spectrum for O = symmetry center and several generic algebraic centers.
conjectures.why_plausible: de Grey's chi>=5 mechanism is exactly a kill of this shape one level down (every 4-coloring of H contains a monochromatic pair in a specified orbit). At 5 colors, Polymath swept within-graph monochromatic distances and failed, but the cross-rotation supply is qualitatively richer: |V|^2 candidate pairs tuned by 3 continuous knobs (center 2, angle 1), and resonances let many pairs constrain simultaneously. The h7 negatives do not bear on this: h7 colored the two copies with independent variables; the diagonal (identified-coloring) instance was never run.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: L57's universal freeness shows lineage 5-colorings are extremely flexible: every non-adjacent pair admits both same and different colors. The same flexibility could let some coloring dodge any FINITE cross set R(rho), for every rho; freeness of single pairs does not imply joint escape, but it is evidence for it.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The diagonal constraint (one coloring shared by graph and rotated copy) appears in no Polymath sweep and no repo experiment; h7/h7b/h7c used free colorings on unions, and shotC/shotD swept single-pair forcing only. Nearest prior art: de Grey 2018's monochromatic-pair lemma (within-graph, 4 colors) and the repo's e1e binding-rotation enumeration (which found the angles but never imposed the diagonal).

conjectures.name: C2: Coloring winding number (imprimitive rotation transport)
conjectures.statement: There exist a chi-5 UDG (G,q), a center O, an integer N >= 3, rho = rotation by 2pi/N about O, an integer m >= 2 with m NOT dividing N, and a map w : C_5(G)/S_5 -> Z/m such that every arc (c,c') of the band-1 transport relation T (pairs of coloring classes compatible with the cross unit edges between q(V) and rho(q(V))) satisfies w(c') = w(c) + 1 mod m. Consequence (a finite, checkable theorem): the necklace Z_N = unit-distance graph on the point set Union_{j=0}^{N-1} rho^j(q(V)) has chi(Z_N) >= 6, because any proper 5-coloring gives a closed band-1 cycle c_0 -> c_1 -> ... -> c_0 whose w-increments sum to N != 0 mod m. Hence chi(R^2) >= 6. The invariant w is a 'winding number' on the 5-coloring set of a UDG, incremented by unit-rotation transport.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: For concrete (G, rho): the transport relation factors through colorings of the ACTIVE BOUNDARY (vertices appearing in cross pairs). Pick resonances with 5-30 cross pairs, enumerate feasible boundary colorings by SAT with blocking clauses (the L55 transfer-matrix machinery, already built), construct the transport digraph on boundary classes, and compute the period (gcd of cycle lengths) of each strongly connected component. Prediction: some swept (G, rho) instance has an SCC of period m >= 2. Refuted-in-sweep if every transport with at least one cycle is aperiodic (period 1). Independently testable consequence: SAT on Z_N directly; UNSAT for any N with the period mismatch is the full chi >= 6 jackpot.
conjectures.why_plausible: Imprimitivity of a wide-interface transfer is the unique surviving combinatorial route (L55 route ii), and this construction is that route with W3 realizability FREE: Z_N is defined as an explicit algebraic point set, so the realizability question that kills the clamp program does not arise. Graded colorings are natural in this problem: the hexagonal 7-coloring carries exactly such a Z/7 translation grading, and Polymath observed quasi-periodic diamond phase structure in 5-colorings of large lineage graphs; a 2pi/N rotation shifts angular phase, the natural source of a Z/m grading. The theory also retrodicts the h7/h7b negatives: OPEN 2- and 3-copy chains compose nonempty relations and stay SAT; the obstruction can only appear when the loop CLOSES at a length the period does not divide. The closed-necklace regime was never tested.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: A grading must be respected by every band-1 arc; 'lazy' arcs (colorings nearly constant near the active boundary transported to themselves) would force period 1. If C1 fails everywhere (transport always has fixed points), C2 fails a fortiori for the swept family, since a fixed point is a cycle of length 1.
conjectures.novelty_claim: A Z/m-valued invariant on the proper-coloring set of a unit-distance graph, transported by the rotation action and obstructing closure, appears nowhere I know of: coloring-complex literature (Hom/box complexes) sees only the abstract graph and is calibrated dead here; tiling-cohomology gradings live on tilings, not coloring sets. Nearest prior art: the repo's own L55 transfer-matrix imprimitivity diagnostic (abstract chains, never geometric rotation orbits) and de Grey's rotated-copy wheel (no transport analysis).

conjectures.name: C3: Chromatically fixed-point-free automorphisms exist in the lineage
conjectures.statement: At least one of the 12 lineage chi-5 UDGs has a nontrivial geometric automorphism sigma (induced by a rotation or reflection symmetry of the exact embedding, e.g. the C_6 symmetry of the de Grey core about v0 = (2,0)) such that for EVERY color permutation pi in S_5 the instance [proper 5-coloring c] + [c(sigma(v)) = pi(c(v)) for all v] is UNSAT. I.e. sigma moves every point of C_5(G)/S_5: no 5-coloring is sigma-symmetric even up to global recoloring.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Per graph: extract geometric symmetries from exact coordinates (no nauty needed; the e1i/e1l symmetry machinery exists), then for each generator sigma and each of the 120 pi (reducible to cycle types compatible with ord(sigma)), one SAT call of the same size as the base graph. Total a few hundred sub-second cadical calls over the 12 graphs. Prediction: at least one (G, sigma) is UNSAT for all pi. Refuted if every (G, sigma) admits a pi-equivariant proper 5-coloring.
conjectures.why_plausible: Equivariant colorings are colorings of the quotient G/sigma with a pi-twist; twisted quotients of odd-girth-rich graphs typically contain forced short monochromatic cycles. de Grey's mechanism already kills the most symmetric 4-colorings of his C_6 core; 5-colorings of the minimized graphs are scarce and contorted, and symmetry is a strong extra constraint. This is the combinatorial half of the twisted-necklace certificate (C4) and is worth knowing in either direction.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Pairing a geometric rotation with a 5-cycle of colors is exactly how lattice colorings achieve equivariance (the hex 7-coloring is rotation-plus-recolor symmetric); some pi may always work. Reflection generators are especially likely to admit symmetric colorings. The SAT-minimized graphs may also have trivial automorphism groups outside their engineered cores.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Equivariant 5-colorability (existence of color-permuting-automorphism-compatible colorings) has never been computed for the de Grey/Polymath lineage; the repo caches contain symmetry GROUPS (e1i, e1l) but no equivariant coloring runs. Nearest prior art: distinguishing chromatic number (Collins-Trenk 2006) and the color-permuting automorphism literature, never applied to Hadwiger-Nelson graphs.

conjectures.name: C4: Twisted necklace closure (theorem) + rigid-transport resonances exist (conjecture)
conjectures.statement: Theorem part (VERIFIER target, elementary): let (G,q) have an order-m rotational symmetry about its own center inducing sigma in Aut(G), let theta = 2pi(m+1)/(Nm) about that center, and copies q_j = rho_theta^j(q), j = 0..N-1; then copy N coincides with copy 0 relabeled by sigma, so the union Z is a finite UDG by construction, and Z is 5-colorable iff there exist coloring classes c_0..c_{N-1} with (c_j, c_{j+1}) in T for j < N-1 and (c_{N-1}, sigma_* c_0) in T. Conjecture part: there exists a resonance rho for some lineage (G, sigma) whose transport is RIGID (every (c,c') in T satisfies c' = pi compose c for some pi in S_5, i.e. cross edges force the rotated copy to repeat the coloring up to recoloring) while sigma is chromatically fixed-point-free (C3). Then Z is UNSAT and chi(R^2) >= 6: rigidity propagates the class around the loop, and the sigma-twist forbids closure.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Rigidity of a candidate transport is SAT-checkable: ask for (c, c') compatible with the cross edges such that c' lies OUTSIDE the S_5-orbit of c (encode via 120 UNSAT subchecks, or incrementally with blocking clauses on the orbit). Prediction: among stacked resonances (angles serving many pairs at once, possibly requiring a flexible armature attached to G to schedule several coincidences simultaneously, since a 1-parameter rotation gives only one knob and a flex with f internal degrees of freedom gives f+1), some transport is rigid. Refuted if transports at all reachable resonances admit orbit-escaping arcs.
conjectures.why_plausible: Wide-interface forced-same-up-to-recoloring is NOT killed by L56/L57: those killed single-PAIR forcing; forcing a whole boundary coloring to repeat up to pi via many simultaneous cross edges is an untested, strictly weaker demand than per-pair forcing (it permits every individual pair to be free, consistent with L57). It is also exactly the imprimitive-block structure route (ii) asks for, in its sharpest form (blocks = S_5-orbits).
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Rigid transport may need more simultaneous cross constraints than any algebraic resonance of a rigid framework supplies; the flexible-armature escalation adds knobs but each armature vertex also adds coloring freedom, a race that could lose. If C3 fails throughout the lineage, the twist has no fuel and the construction needs a new G.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The closed sigma-twisted rotation necklace, with UNSAT reduced to (rigid transport) AND (chromatically fixed-point-free sigma), has to my knowledge never been stated; it is the discrete shadow of coloring monodromy along a loop in the framework configuration space. Nearest prior art: Moser spindling (N = 2, untwisted, single shared vertex), de Grey's rotated wheel (untwisted, no transport analysis), repo h7b 3-orbits (open chains, free colorings).

detector_self_check.Q2: The mechanism's fuel is (a) a chi-5 core G with odd-cycle-rich structure and (b) resonance angles theta solving |q(u) - rho_theta(q(v))| = 1, which exist by the intermediate value theorem on a continuous circle of placements, i.e. by completeness of R, and are algebraically irrational, so the necklace's coordinates leave Q^2. Applied to Q^2 the idea is inert twice over: the realization set of a framework over Q^2 is totally disconnected (no flex paths, no loops, trivial monodromy), and the Q^2 unit-distance graph is bipartite with a parity 2-coloring that is invariant under every Q^2-preserving rotation, so every transport relation contains the canonical fixed point and every necklace stays 2-colorable. The idea cannot prove chi(Q^2) >= 3.
detector_self_check.Linf: Three Euclidean-specific rigidities are load-bearing. First, the loop is SO(2): the L^infty isometry group is signed coordinate permutations times translations, with no continuous rotation family, so the base space of the monodromy has no rotation loops and the angle knob theta does not exist. Second, resonances are isolated codimension-1 events because two Euclidean unit circles meet in <= 2 points; in L^infty unit spheres meet in segments, cross-incidences are positive-dimensional, and the transport relation degenerates. Third, the periodic 4-coloring of the L^infty plane is invariant under the translations that replace rotations there, providing a canonical transport fixed point, so closure never obstructs. The idea cannot push the L^infty plane above 4.
detector_self_check.R1: The construction is built ON the rotation group: the necklace IS an SO(2)-orbit discretization and the conjectured invariant w is an angular winding number, so the method is maximally rotation-aware, not rotation-blind. On R^1 the placement space of a framework is finite (each edge fixes relative position up to sign), pi_1 is trivial, the only 'rotation' is the reflection x -> -x, and the parity 2-coloring of the line is transport-invariant, so all transports have fixed points and no closure obstruction can arise. The idea cannot constrain chi(R^1).

experiment.description: Resonance census + diagonal kill screen + closed necklaces on S199 (smallest lineage chi-5 graph, 199 vtx, 888 edges; data in c:/Users/Owen/dev/hadwiger-nelson/sources/cnp-sat/vtx/ and c:/Users/Owen/dev/hadwiger-nelson/experiments/combinatorial/_cache/shotD/S199.summary.json). Stage 1: for O = the graph's symmetry center plus 2 generic algebraic centers, compute all angles theta with |q(u) - rho_theta(q(v))| = 1 over all ~40k ordered pairs (numpy vectorized two-circle solve at 50-digit mpmath, then sympy exact certification of clustered coincidences, reusing the binding-rotation system in c:/Users/Owen/dev/hadwiger-nelson/experiments/combinatorial/e1e_binding_rotation_moser.py). Rank angles by simultaneous cross-pair multiplicity. Stage 2 (tests C1): for the top ~100 resonant angles, one diagonal SAT call each: 5-coloring of S199 on ONE variable set plus x_u != x_v for every cross pair; UNSAT anywhere validates the monodromy program at step one. Stage 3 (tests C2's consequence directly): for theta = 2pi/N, N in {4,5,6,8,10,12} (quadratic/quartic cos fields, sympy-exact), build the full necklace Z_N (all bands, <= 2388 vertices, exact unit-distance edge detection) and SAT it with cadical, dual-confirming any UNSAT with glucose; in parallel, for 3 resonances with 5-30 cross pairs, enumerate boundary colorings with blocking clauses and compute transport SCC periods (the L55 transfer-matrix code path). Decision rule: any UNSAT in Stage 2 or 3 = breakthrough candidate, escalate to VERIFIER; any SCC period >= 2 = direct support for C2, escalate to necklace tuning with N coprime to the period; all-SAT and all-aperiodic = clean logged negative ('rotation transport on the lineage always has fixed points and period 1'), redirecting the program to flexible armatures or new cores. ADVERSARY targets: attempt 5-colorings of the best Z_N by hand-guided local search; check Stage 1 exactness by independent sympy re-certification of 10 random resonances; rerun Stage 2 UNSATs with assumption-based minimal cores.
experiment.repo_feasibility: Everything runs on the existing stack: python-sat (cadical/glucose) for all coloring decisions, sympy for exact resonance certification in Q(sqrt3,sqrt11)(cos theta), numpy/mpmath for the numeric prefilter; no compiler, no nauty needed (symmetries come from exact coordinates as in the e1i cache). Graph sizes (199 to 2388 vtx) are inside the repo's routine SAT envelope; the transfer-matrix and blocking-clause enumeration code paths exist from L54-L55.
experiment.est_cost: Roughly half a day of single-machine compute: minutes for the numeric census, ~1-2 hours of sympy certification, ~100 sub-second diagonal SAT calls, 6 necklace builds at minutes each (exact edge detection dominates), plus ~1 hour for three transport-period computations. One BUILDER session end to end.

result.first_principles: The plane offers exactly one primitive 5-color constraint: the unit edge, forced-different at distance 1. The frontier results say this primitive never amplifies locally: L45/L56/L57 prove the entire lineage has no nonlocal pair forcing, and L55 proves single-vertex channels carry nothing beyond same/diff/free. Conclusion from first principles: a chi >= 6 certificate cannot be a local forcing statement at all. It must be a GLOBAL inconsistency among locally free coloring data. The mathematical home of locally-free-globally-impossible is monodromy: a locally constant structure over a base with nontrivial loops, whose holonomy admits no invariant section. The correct base is the configuration space of placements of a finite framework (G,q); the fiber over a placement is its finite set of proper 5-colorings. Fibers are locally constant but get cut on resonance strata where incidental unit distances appear, and Euclidean two-circle rigidity makes those strata isolated, codimension 1. Transporting coloring classes along a loop, through chosen strata, composes inequality relations into a transport relation on C_5(G)/S_5. If the loop closes geometrically (N rotation steps returning the framework to itself, possibly relabeled by sigma) while the composed relation misses the sigma-twisted diagonal, the closed object is an EXPLICIT finite point set whose unit-distance graph has chi >= 6. The clamp program's entire difficulty (W3 realizability) evaporates: the certificate is born realized; all burden moves to combinatorics, which is SAT-decidable. This is precisely L55's route (ii), wide interface plus imprimitive transfer, with the geometry supplying both the interface (cross resonance edges) and the imprimitivity candidate (an angular Z/m grading of colorings, the winding number). The structure lives exactly where R^2 differs from the controls: the loop is SO(2) (absent in L^infty), resonance angles exist by completeness (absent in Q^2), and placement spaces on R^1 have trivial pi_1. The theory even retrodicts the repo's h7/h7b negatives: open 2- and 3-copy rotation chains compose nonempty relations and must stay 5-colorable; only CLOSED necklaces of period-mismatched length can be empty, and those were never built.
result.lens: Equivariant topology on configuration spaces: coloring monodromy of unit-distance frameworks, discretized as twisted rotation necklaces
